name: Neuralink N1
description: A fully wireless, implantable brain-computer interface that records from 1024 electrodes inserted into motor cortex. The N1 chip processes neural data on-device and transmits decoded intent wirelessly to computers, enabling paralyzed patients to control digital devices with thought.

name: Stentrode
description: The Stentrode is a stent-electrode array delivered through blood vessels to the motor cortex without open-brain surgery. Deployed via the jugular vein, it records local field potentials from within the superior sagittal sinus and wirelessly transmits signals to an implanted device in the chest.

name: Muse S (Gen 2)
description: A lightweight consumer-grade EEG headband designed for meditation and sleep tracking. Muse S uses 4 scalp electrodes (Fp1, Fp2, TP9, TP10) plus three reference sensors to detect frontal alpha and beta oscillations in real time, providing audio biofeedback during mindfulness practice.

name: Ultracortex Mark IV
description: An open-source, 3D-printable EEG headset compatible with OpenBCI's Cyton and Daisy biosensing boards. The Ultracortex Mark IV supports up to 16 channels of EEG recording and is designed for DIY neuroscience, BCI research, and hackathons. All hardware files and firmware are freely available on GitHub.

name: Kernel Flow
description: A wearable, helmet-like functional near-infrared spectroscopy (fNIRS) system with 52 modules and custom ASICs. Flow measures hemodynamic responses across the cortex at millisecond timescales and outputs continuous oxy/deoxy-hemoglobin data, enabling naturalistic neuroimaging outside MRI scanners.

name: EPOC X
description: The Emotiv EPOC X features 14 gold-plated saline-based sensors arranged according to the 10-20 system and provides wireless EEG recording at 256 Hz. It supports cognitive state detection and facial expression classification through Emotiv's cloud-based mental command suite.

name: Neuroport Array
description: The Neuroport (formerly BrainGate) is the gold-standard intracortical silicon microelectrode array used in most human BCI clinical trials. A 4×4 mm silicon base houses 96 needles (1.5 mm long) coated with platinum or iridium oxide tips for reliable spike and LFP recording.

name: Medtronic Percept PC
description: The Percept PC is the first FDA-approved DBS system with on-board local field potential sensing. It delivers continuous therapeutic stimulation to subcortical targets (STN, GPi, Vim) for Parkinson's disease and essential tremor while simultaneously recording LFP biomarkers, enabling closed-loop and adaptive DBS.

name: MagVenture MagPro X100
description: A high-performance repetitive transcranial magnetic stimulation (rTMS) system cleared by the FDA for major depressive disorder. Delivers up to 100 Hz pulse trains through interchangeable coils. Supports theta-burst stimulation protocols and can be paired with neuronavigation for precise target localization.

name: BrainCo Focus 1
description: The Focus 1 is a single-channel EEG headband aimed at educational settings. It reads frontal alpha and beta power to infer a student's 'focus score' in real time, which teachers can monitor through a class dashboard. Designed for classrooms in China and the United States.

name: Axon HD
description: The Orion cortical visual prosthesis system (successor to Argus II retinal implant) stimulated primary visual cortex to elicit phosphenes in blind individuals. After Second Sight's bankruptcy in 2022, remaining implanted patients were left without support, raising ongoing ethical concerns about long-term device responsibility.
